campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth with recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a safer bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donating $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Extending temporary local ordinance #2326
Planning a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Applying for state grant funding for the Hamilton Avenue bike bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, they plan to introduce an ordinance (a new city law) and move it forward for final approval at the next meeting, skipping some of the usual procedural steps to speed up the process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance being introduced
Fast-tracked approval process (waived first reading)
Final vote expected at next council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details were cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of multiple March meeting minutes from Town Council and study sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc. (project details incomplete)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve several updates to city contracts and projects. The main items included fixing the airport's baggage system through a deal with Vanderlande Industries, protecting affordable housing when subsidies end, updating the storm sewer system plan with Wood Rodgers, and moving forward with the FEMA Family Camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade agreement
Affordable housing protections when subsidies expire
Storm sewer system city-wide master planning
FEMA San José Family Camp project
Various contract amendments and approvals

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including infrastructure and improvement projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: Budget review for city construction and improvement projects in 2026-27
Teen Month proclamation recognizing local youth
National Volunteer Week recognition for community service contributions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting opened with the traditional flag salute and featured these observances as special items on the agenda.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular city council business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows the public where the city's money comes from and where it goes. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city—meaning those who try to influence city decisions—to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget and spending visibility
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
Rules for people trying to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for Santa Clara residents. If you disagree with a city decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from when the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit—this is a strict deadline you shouldn't miss.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 enforcement

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to review several administrative matters and make decisions about the city's spending plans and council operations. Key items included approving changes to a federal housing and community development funding plan, reviewing new project requests from individual council members, and considering a change to how the city fills council vacancies.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development spending plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Proposed changes to the council vacancy filling process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and confirmation of upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The building's owner is asking to upgrade its historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space as a reward for preserving and fixing up the historic structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building getting upgraded historic status
5,000 square feet of bonus space approved for renovation and seismic safety improvements
Located in downtown commercial district
Historic Resources Board minutes from March meeting reviewed
